name: Kentucky Bourbon Brown-Sugar Pound Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 Cup margarine or butter, (2 sticks), softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees F. Grease and flour a 12-cup fluted tube pan.
In a medium bowl, combine flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
In a 1-cup glass measuring cup, combine milk, vanilla, and 4 tablespoons bourbon.
2. In a large bowl, with mixer on medium speed, beat brown sugar and 1/2 cup
sugar until free of lumps. Add margarine or butter and beat at high speed
until light and creamy, about 5 minutes. Add eggs, one at a time,
beating well after each addition. On low speed, alternately add flour
mixture and milk mixture, beginning and ending with the flour mixture.
3. Pour batter into pan. Bake for 1 hour and 20 minutes or until the cake
springs back when lightly touched with a finger and a toothpick inserted in
the center comes out clean. Cool the cake in the pan on a wire rack for
10 minutes. Remove the cake from the pan.
4. In a small bowl, combine orange juice, 1/3 cup sugar, and remaining 2
tablespoons bourbon; brush mixture over the warm cake. Allow the cake to
cool completely before serving. Garnish with berries if desired.
